name: Matillion API
what_it_does: Provides REST API access to Matillion's cloud-native ELT platform for triggering, monitoring, and managing data transformation jobs, environments, and pipeline orchestration on cloud data warehouses.
use_cases: Trigger a Matillion pipeline run programmatically after a source data file lands in S3 or GCS to kick off downstream transformations
Poll job execution status and surface pipeline success or failure to an orchestration agent (e.g., Airflow, Prefect) as part of a larger DAG
Parameterize and run different pipeline variants by passing environment variables to a Matillion job at runtime
Automate promotion of pipeline definitions from development to production environments via API-driven export/import
Monitor job run history to detect recurring failures and trigger alerts or remediation workflows
not_for: Ad-hoc SQL or data querying — Matillion's API controls pipeline orchestration, not the data warehouse query layer
Lightweight or low-cost data integration — Matillion is enterprise-priced and runs on cloud data warehouse infrastructure
Teams without Snowflake, Redshift, BigQuery, or Azure Synapse — Matillion is purpose-built for cloud data warehouses only
best_when: An agent needs to orchestrate Matillion ELT jobs as part of a larger data pipeline, particularly when triggering runs from external events or integrating with an existing workflow orchestrator.
avoid_when: The data integration need is simple or the team lacks a cloud data warehouse — Matillion's complexity and cost are overkill for non-enterprise ELT workloads.

auth.notes: Matillion ETL (legacy) uses HTTP Basic Auth (username:password) per instance. Matillion Data Productivity Cloud uses API keys or OAuth 2.0 client credentials. Basic auth over HTTPS for legacy instances; credentials should be stored securely and not embedded in pipeline configs.

pricing.paid_tiers: Matillion Data Productivity Cloud pricing is consumption-based (credits per job run) with platform fees; Matillion ETL is licensed per instance. Pricing requires direct sales engagement.
pricing.notes: Enterprise-tier pricing. No public pricing listed. Typically requires annual contracts. API access is included with any Matillion subscription.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; API is designed for orchestration use cases, not high-frequency polling. Recommend polling job status no more than once per 10 seconds.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses vary between Matillion ETL and Data Productivity Cloud versions. ETL returns JSON with status and message; DPC follows a more consistent REST pattern. Documentation of error codes is incomplete.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Job trigger calls create new executions each time — not idempotent. Agents must track whether a job is already running before triggering to avoid duplicate runs.
known_agent_gotchas: The API surface differs significantly between Matillion ETL (legacy, instance-based) and Matillion Data Productivity Cloud (new SaaS) — confirm which product is deployed before building integrations
Job runs are asynchronous — the trigger endpoint returns immediately; agents must poll the execution status endpoint with a job run ID to determine completion
Basic auth credentials for Matillion ETL are per-instance and per-environment; dev and prod instances have separate credential sets that must be managed independently
Job parameter passing is environment-variable based; variable names must exactly match what is defined in the Matillion job, with no validation error if a variable is misspelled
Long-running jobs can exceed default HTTP timeout settings; agents should use long-poll or status-check patterns rather than holding connections open

security_score_components.security_notes: Basic auth is weak if credentials are stored insecurely. Matillion DPC's OAuth client credentials are significantly stronger. No IP allowlisting documented for legacy ETL instances. Recommend rotating credentials regularly.
